name: How do I know if structured data generation is actually paying off for my business?
description: Measure ROI by tracking rich-result impressions, click-through rates, AI answer visibility, and conversions on pages with newly generated structured data versus

mainEntity.name: How do I know if structured data generation is actually paying off for my business?

acceptedAnswer.text:
To measure ROI from structured data generation, track changes in rich-result impressions, click-through rates, AI answer inclusion, and downstream conversions or revenue on marked-up pages.
Treat it like any other optimization, with baselines and experiments.

Key factors:
- Before/after metrics for templates with new schema
- Segmentation by device, location, and query type
- Attribution assumptions (schema vs. other changes)
- Time window long enough to smooth out volatility

Practically, pick a control group of similar pages without new schema and compare performance against pages where you deploy generated structured data.
Use dashboards and annotations so you can see when changes went live.
If you don’t see meaningful impact, adjust your schema focus or accept that some content types gain more than others.
acceptedAnswer.description: Measure ROI by tracking rich-result impressions, click-through rates, AI answer visibility, and conversions on pages with newly generated structured data versus similar pages without it. Use a clear before/after window and control group so you can attribute performance changes with confidence.
